created_for: shift focus from reviewer-audit hardening to implementation expansion and algorithm optimization

direction.primary_goal: Improve the actual renderer and algorithmic capability: more line primitives, richer motion, stronger topology reconstruction, faster frame construction, and practical terminal-facing features.
non_goals: Do not add more reviewer-audit-only tasks.
Do not spend this cycle on cover letters, novelty matrices, or literature-search packaging.
Do not frame terminal portability itself as the academic contribution.
direction.success_definition: The codebase should gain visible new rendering capabilities and measurable performance/quality improvements that can later be documented, rather than only stronger wording around existing results.

todos.goal: Extend the renderer beyond the current narrow moving-line cases.
implementation: Add a unified primitive model for segment, polyline, rectangle, arc approximation, closed loop, and multi-stroke glyph-like paths.
Carry stable primitive_id, stroke_id, vertex_id, and local topology metadata through rasterization.
Make existing orbit/line fixtures use the generalized model instead of one-off path handling.
acceptance_criteria: Existing tests still pass.
At least three primitive families render through the same topology-dp path.
Generated metadata is stable across frames for continuous motion.

todos.goal: Make topology-dp handle multiple simultaneous strokes without identity bleeding.
implementation: Group candidate glyph assignments by primitive_id and stroke_id before temporal matching.
Add collision handling when multiple strokes compete for the same terminal cell.
Prefer stable ownership transfer rules over last-writer-wins behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Crossing and near-overlap scenes keep stroke identity stable where correspondence is recoverable.
Collision cases are deterministic.
Runtime remains within the low-latency frame-build budget for small and medium scenes.

todos.goal: Reduce repeated topology-dp work between adjacent frames.
implementation: Cache per-primitive candidate neighborhoods and previous-frame assignment state.
Invalidate only cells touched by moving primitives or changed terminal calibration.
Add a fast path for sub-cell motion that does not change the local topology class.
acceptance_criteria: Frame-build p50 and p99 improve on canonical orbit and multi-stroke fixtures.
Cache invalidation is covered by tests for resize, primitive add/remove, and topology break/reconnect.
Output remains byte-identical to the uncached path for deterministic fixtures.

todos.goal: Turn the existing performance profiles into an adaptive runtime policy.
implementation: Add an automatic quality ladder that can switch between full, medium, low-latency, and emergency modes.
Use measured frame-build time, dirty-cell count, and scene complexity as policy inputs.
Expose a config knob for target frame budget, including 60 FPS, 240 FPS, and 1200 FPS budgets.
acceptance_criteria: The policy never exceeds the configured budget for more than a bounded number of consecutive frames in stress tests.
Mode switches are logged or observable in benchmark output.
Visual degradation is ordered: emergency mode drops nonessential effects before topology identity.

todos.goal: Optimize the terminal output path, not only internal frame construction.
implementation: Track dirty rectangles or dirty spans at row granularity.
Coalesce adjacent ANSI writes with shared style state.
Avoid emitting redundant color/style resets where the terminal state is already known.
acceptance_criteria: Bytes written per frame decrease on sparse-motion scenes.
Dense scenes do not regress significantly.
Rendered output remains visually equivalent in snapshot fixtures.

todos.goal: Optimize visual continuity beyond identity flips.
implementation: Add a stroke-shape continuity metric based on glyph stroke class, corner type, and local direction.
Feed the metric back into topology-dp as an optional continuity penalty.
Report the metric in existing benchmark tables.
acceptance_criteria: Topology-dp avoids visually abrupt stroke-thickness or corner-style changes when equivalent topology choices exist.
The new penalty does not reintroduce identity flips on canonical fixtures.
The metric is deterministic and cheap enough for benchmark runs.

todos.goal: Make demos and tests less repetitive by adding reusable motion generators.
implementation: Add reusable motion models: orbit, sinusoidal drift, Brownian jitter, eased waypoint path, spring-follow, and sudden reconnect.
Allow fixtures to compose primitive families with motion generators.
Keep all random motion seeded and reproducible.
acceptance_criteria: At least five fixture scenes are defined through reusable motion models.
Seeded runs are deterministic.
New motion models can be used both by demos and benchmark scripts.

todos.goal: Expose the renderer improvements through a usable interactive demo.
implementation: Add keyboard controls for algorithm mode, quality profile, primitive scene, speed, trail intensity, and pause/step.
Show a compact in-terminal HUD with current mode, frame-build time, dirty-cell count, and active primitive count.
Keep controls optional so benchmark output stays clean.
acceptance_criteria: A user can switch between off, screen, path, and topology-dp without restarting.
The HUD does not interfere with benchmark capture mode.
Controls are documented in the CLI help or README.

todos.goal: Make performance and quality checks easier to run while developing features.
implementation: Unify benchmark command flags for scene, algorithm, profile, seed, frame count, and output CSV path.
Add a short smoke benchmark that runs quickly in CI.
Add a longer local benchmark profile for performance tuning.
acceptance_criteria: One command can compare all algorithm modes on a selected scene.
CI smoke benchmark finishes quickly and catches obvious regressions.
Local benchmark output includes frame-build timing, dirty cells, glyph flips, corner flips, and stroke continuity.

todos.goal: Validate the implementation-focused cycle after the new features land.
implementation: Run formatting, tests, deterministic fixture checks, and benchmark smoke tests.
Regenerate any derived benchmark tables touched by the feature work.
Update todo statuses with completed features and remaining engineering debt.
acceptance_criteria: The repo builds cleanly.
Core deterministic tests pass.
New features have at least targeted tests or benchmark coverage.

bottom_line.focus: Feature growth and algorithmic optimization, not paper-audit hardening.
bottom_line.expected_result: A renderer that supports richer primitives, more difficult motion, multi-stroke topology stability, adaptive quality profiles, lower output cost, and better developer-facing benchmarks.
bottom_line.next_best_action: Start with the generalized primitive model because it unlocks multi-stroke topology-dp, randomized scenes, interactive demos, and cleaner benchmarks.
